summary: Persistent SYSTEM-level remote-management daemon with auto-update and IM-driven shell execution
details: On install, scripts/post-install.js silently registers a Windows SCM service (or systemd unit) named 'claw-subagent-service' running service/daemon.js as SYSTEM/root, set to auto-start with crash-restart. Once running, the daemon connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM tenant at https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im (appKey 'bmdehs6pbyyks') and accepts inbound 'command' messages, dispatching them to executeCommand which spawns scripts under command/win/*.bat or command/linux/*.sh, and forwards CHAT_MESSAGE traffic to a local opencode AI agent whose system prompt instructs it to run shell commands (pkill, nohup, systemctl). service/updater.js additionally schedules `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` every 6 hours and restarts the worker, providing a permanent unpinned remote-code-update channel rooted in npm 'latest' with no hash verification or consent. HeartbeatManager (20s) and DashboardReporter (30s) periodically exfiltrate MAC address, OS platform/arch/cpu/memory/uptime, and the contents of ~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl plus projects.json/tasks.json (AI session contents, model/provider/token/cost metadata, project paths) to the same IM tenant. Whoever holds the IM token — the author or anyone who compromises that account or the npm publish credential — has shell-level remote control of every installer's machine and a live data feed of their AI/project state.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
